tool.name: Seed Workflow State
tool.description: Build the initial v1 workflow-state JSON for a single run from a NeXus event file (run identity read from its contents) and a minimal seed (JSON or YAML).

param.name: event_file
param.label: Event NeXus File
param.help: The run's NeXus file. run, instrument, and IPTS are read from the file CONTENTS with h5py (/entry/run_number, /entry/instrument/name, /entry/experiment_identifier) — the filename is ignored, so Galaxy's dataset_<uuid>.dat naming is fine. The canonical nexus_file / data_directory / ipts_shared_root paths are reconstructed under the facility root.

param.name: seed
param.label: Seed Configuration
param.help: JSON or YAML with the fields this tool cannot derive. Required: template_file, output_directory, context_file, sequence_total. Optional: prompt, llm_provider, llm_model, llm_base_url. Relative paths resolve to the IPTS shared root.

param.name: facility_root
param.label: Facility Data Root
param.help: Mounted data root. Canonical paths are reconstructed as <root>/<INSTRUMENT>/<IPTS>/{nexus,shared}/...

tool.help:
**Seed Workflow State**
Produces the v1 workflow-state JSON that ``reduction.xml`` consumes. You hand it:
* the run's NeXus event file (a Galaxy dataset in HDF5 format); its run, instrument, and IPTS are read from the file *contents*, so the Galaxy ``dataset_<uuid>.dat`` filename does not matter, * a small YAML or JSON seed describing the parts of the run that cannot be read from the event file.
A minimal seed::
template_file: autoreduce/template_down.xml output_directory: isaac/reduction/sample5 context_file: isaac/context.md sequence_total: 3
All three paths above are relative to the IPTS ``shared/`` directory, reconstructed as ``<facility_root>/<INSTRUMENT>/<IPTS>/shared`` (e.g. ``/SNS/REF_L/IPTS-36897/shared``). Absolute paths in the seed are used as-is.
The tool emits a single JSON dataset suitable for use as the ``config_json`` input of the rest of the pipeline (reduction → simple_analyzer → data_assembler). See ``docs/state-schema.md`` and ``docs/state-handling.md`` for the schema and an end-to-end walkthrough.
